entry.title: Cloud ALM を使用したリアルユーザ監視の方法について
entry.content:
ご担当者様

お世話になっております。

SAP Cloud ALM の導入を検討しています。
例えばリアルユーザ監視を使用して、SAP GUI および Fiori で応答時間が一定時間以上の場合、
担当者にメールで通知するというような監視を設定したい場合、
どのように設定すれば良いか、下記デモテナントで実施可能な具体的な手順をご教授いただけますでしょうか。

よろしくお願いいたします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM を使用したリアルユーザ監視の方法について
entry.content:
ご担当者様、
お世話になっております。
　
恐れ入りますが、デモテナント環境用として提供されているユーザは、データの表示のみ可能なユーザとなり、監視設定の追加や変更等に関する権限は持っておりません。

リアルユーザ監視(RUM)の設定に関する詳細は、下記のドキュメントよりご確認頂けますのでご参照頂ければと思います。
・https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/real-user-monitoring/run-details.html
・https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/real-user-monitoring
・3459306 - How to configure SAP Cloud ALM Real User Monitoring alerting and e-mail notifications for HTTP errors?
　
尚、RUMの設定の際にエラー等が発生しましたら、現在ご利用のCloud ALM製品をご選択いただき、コンポーネントSV-CLM-OP-RUMとしてケースを起票してくださいますようお願いいたします。
　
以上、よろしくお願いいたします。
SAPサポート

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition
entry.content:
Hi @triciajohnson,

Thanks for this very informative blog. It’s great to see the broader community getting visibility into this new edition of SAP Integration Suite, and the strong response in the comments is a good sign that the topic is generating a lot of interest.

I have a quick question and hope you might be able to clarify. If a client subscribes to the enhanced edition for the development environment, would the same edition also be required for the test and production environments? Or would it still be possible to transport iFlows from dev to test and prod even if those environments use different editions?

I’m also thinking about how best to position the AI capabilities across environments. For example, using this edition in dev environment could be valuable so the integration team can leverage the AI features for generating iFlows. However, considering the 500k message limit and the additional AI features for APIM, it might feel like an inefficient use of cost in a dev environment where transaction volumes are typically low.

On the other hand, if the enhanced edition is only used in production while non-prod environments run on the standard edition, teams may hesitate to use AI-assisted iFlow generation or script optimization directly in production. 

Curious to hear your thoughts on how SAP envisions customers positioning these AI features across dev, test, and production environments.

Thanks again for sharing the insights.

 

Regards,
Nick

entry.title: Re: #CoE#TFAL_S/4 HANA public cloud 2602 connection /inte...
entry.content:
Hello,

Please check following integration options with SailPoints, FYI.

Option 1: Direct Integration via SailPoint Connectors

SailPoint provides native connectors (Identity 8.4/8.5) that can directly integrate with S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using standard APIs and communication scenarios.

Supported Authentication Methods:

  • OAuth 2.0 with client credentials
  • OAuth 2.0 SAML Bearer Assertion
  • Basic Authentication (not recommended for production)

Key Capabilities:

  • User aggregation from S/4HANA Cloud
  • Automated provisioning and deprovisioning
  • Business role assignment and revocation
  • Real-time or scheduled synchronization
  • Support for custom attributes and extensions

Limitations:

  • Requires proper communication scenario setup in S/4HANA Cloud
  • Limited to released APIs and standard objects
  • May require custom connector configuration for specific use cases
Option 2: API Integration via SAP IAS (Identity Authentication Service)

SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) acts as an intermediary identity provider and provisioning hub, enabling SailPoint to integrate with S/4HANA Cloud through standardized protocols.

Integration Flow:

  1. SailPoint connects to SAP IAS using SCIM 2.0 protocol
  2. SAP Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) synchronizes users from IAS to S/4HANA Cloud
  3. Authentication flows through IAS for single sign-on (SSO)

API Protocols Supported:

  • SCIM 2.0 for user provisioning
  • OAuth 2.0 for authentication
  • SAML 2.0 for SSO

Key Capabilities:

  • Centralized identity management across multiple SAP and non-SAP systems
  • Standardized SCIM-based provisioning
  • Built-in SSO capabilities
  • Identity lifecycle management
  • Support for corporate identity provider integration

Limitations:

  • Additional layer of complexity
  • Requires SAP IAS tenant and configuration
  • May introduce latency in provisioning workflows
  • Limited direct control over S/4HANA-specific attributes
Option 3: Other Integration Patterns

Hybrid Approach:

  • Use SAP IAS for authentication and basic user provisioning
  • Use direct SailPoint connector for advanced role management and entitlements
  • Leverage SAP Master Data Integration Service for HR-driven provisioning

Custom API Integration:

  • Build custom integration using S/4HANA Cloud released APIs
  • Implement via SAP BTP Integration Suite or middleware
  • Suitable for highly customized requirements

entry.title: Re: #CoE#TFAL_S/4 HANA public cloud 2602 connection /inte...
entry.content:
Hello,

Following please find the configuration on S/4(without IAS),

 S/4HANA Public Cloud Configuration

  1. Enable Required Communication Scenarios

    Navigate to Communication Arrangements app in S/4HANA Cloud:

    • Create arrangement for SAP_COM_0193 (Business User - Read)
    • Create arrangement for SAP_COM_0276 (Business User - Write)
    • Create arrangement for SAP_COM_0532 (Business Role - Read)

  2. Create Communication System

    In Communication Systems app:

    • Create new communication system (e.g., SAILPOINT_SYSTEM)
    • Set host name to SailPoint instance URL
    • Enable OAuth 2.0 authentication
    • Configure OAuth 2.0 client ID and secret

  3. Create Communication Arrangement

    In Communication Arrangements app:

    • Select communication scenario (e.g., SAP_COM_0193)
    • Assign communication system created in step 2
    • Configure service URL and authentication details
    • Activate the arrangement

  4. Configure OAuth 2.0 Client

    In Maintain OAuth 2.0 Clients app:

    • Create OAuth 2.0 client for SailPoint
    • Grant type: Client Credentials or SAML Bearer Assertion
    • Assign required scopes (e.g., API_BUSINESS_USER_READ, API_BUSINESS_USER_WRITE)
    • Note client ID and secret for SailPoint configuration

  5. Set Up Technical User with Required Authorizations

    In Maintain Business Users app:

    • Create technical user (e.g., SAILPOINT_TECH)
    • Assign business role with communication management authorizations
    • Ensure user has read/write access to business users and roles
    • Test user login and API access

  6. Configure Business Role Assignments

    In Maintain Business Roles app:

    • Review existing business roles
    • Document role descriptions and purposes
    • Ensure roles are properly structured for external governance
    • Configure restrictions (e.g., company code, cost center) as needed

entry.title: STO Movement between Storage Locations
entry.content:
While working with SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM), one interesting design consideration arises when transportation planning is required between storage locations within the same plant.

Since the planning engine in SAP TM relies heavily on location modeling, it is important that the system can clearly differentiate the source and destination. One effective approach is to model Plant–Storage Location combinations as separate TM locations, allowing the system to recognize an actual transportation movement.

In some cases, a custom enhancement may also be required to ensure that both the source and destination locations in Freight Units or Freight Orders are derived correctly based on the storage location information.

This highlights how master data modeling and small design decisions can significantly influence transportation planning behavior in SAP TM.

Always interesting to see how thoughtful design can simplify complex logistics scenarios.

#SAP #SAPTM #SAPCommunity #SupplyChain #Logistics

entry.title: Superpackage ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT is not a valid package
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Using the Developer Extensibility model, we created several custom objects. At the start of development, the structure package ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT was not available in the TDD system (Partner Demo Development Tenant), so we used the structure package ZPARTNER and completed the development.

Now, while preparing to transport these objects to QA, we are encountering the error: “Software component of transport is inconsistent.” The ZPARTNER package currently has no transport layer assigned.

Since the ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT structure package is now available, I attempted to move our development packages under it. However, this results in the error: “Superpackage ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT is not a valid package.”

What options are available to move these objects to the QA system?

Regards,
Karthik

entry.title: How can employees be made ineligible for Variable Pay based on a lookup value?
entry.content: Our client does not maintain an ineligible flag in Employee Central (EC). Instead, they prefer to upload the data through a lookup table using EMPLID and an Eligible Flag, where only ineligible employees are included (e.g., 10001 | N). Based on this information, these employees should either be excluded from Employee History or completely removed from the form.

entry.title: CDS Custom Entities
entry.content:
Introduction

A CDS custom entity is to implement own data retrieval using ABAP.

A CDS custom entity is linked with an ABAP class, and the data retrieval is executed on the application server.

Custom entities are used for data models whose runtime is implemented manually.

At the CDS level, only an interface is defined.

Runtime of a CDS Custom Entity

It shows that the data retrieval is implemented by an ABAP class and the data source does not have to be a database table.

CDS custom entities transient entities, which means they aren't created as a database object and can't be accessed using ABAP SQL.

In ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), custom entities are used as a data model basis for RAP unmanaged queries, the ABAP query is executed by the RAP query engine.

A CDS custom entity cannot be used as a data source in other CDS entities or in ABAP SQL.

Syntax

// optional header annotations
@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy : 'ClassName'
define [root] custom entity EntityName
[with parameters ...]
{
  [field]
  [association]
  [annotation]
}

Creating a CDS Custom Entity

Defining the Data Model in a CDS Custom Entity

A custom entity defines an element list that can consist of elements or associations to other CDS entities.

It binds an ABAP class with annotation @ObjectModel.query.implementedBy.

Annotations and input parameters are optional.

The element list of a custom entity can contain single fields or CDS associations:

  • Individual fields have a name and a data type. The data type can be specified using a CDS built-in type, a CDS simple type, a CDS enumerated type, or a DDIC data element.
  • Associations can be regular associations or specialized associations, i.e. to-parent associations or to-child associations. They can be used to define a relationship to another table entity, thereby adding the association target as an additional data source.
  • Annotations at the element level can be used to add semantic information, such as text labels.
@EndUserText.label: 'PO Header'
define custom entity ZI_PO_HEAD
 with parameters parameter_name : parameter_type
{
  key key_element_name : key_element_type;
  element_name : element_types;
}

Header Entity

@EndUserText.label: 'PO Header' 
define custom entity ZI_PO_HEAD
//with parameters parameter_name: parameter_type
{
   key po_num : ebeln;
   doc_cat    : bstyp;
   type       : abap.char(2);
   org        : ekorg;
   comp_code  : bukrs;
   status     : abap.char(1);
   vendor     : Lifnrs
   plant      : werks_d;
}

Item Entity

@EndUserText.label: 'PO Items'
define custom entity ZI_PO_ITS
// with parameters parameter_name : parameter_type
{
   key po num    : ebeln;
   key po_item   : ebelp;
   item_text     : abap.char (40);
   material      : abap.char(40);
   plant         : werks_d;
   stor_loc      : Lgort_d;
   @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'uom
   qty           : abap.quan(13,3);
   uom           : abap.unit (3);
   @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'price_unit'
   product_price : abap.curr(10,2);
   price_unit    : abap.cuky;
}

Header Entity as Root Entity

@EndUserText.label: 'PO Header' 
define root custom entity ZI_PO_HEAD
//with parameters parameter_name: parameter_type
{
   key po_num : ebeln;
   doc_cat    : bstyp;
   type       : abap.char(2);
   org        : ekorg;
   comp_code  : bukrs;
   status     : abap.char(1);
   vendor     : Lifnrs
   plant      : werks_d;
}

Relationships in CDS Custom Entity

@EndUserText.label: 'PO Header' 
define root custom entity ZI_PO_HEAD
//with parameters parameter_name: parameter_type
{
   key po_num : ebeln;
   doc_cat    : bstyp;
   type       : abap.char(2);
   org        : ekorg;
   comp_code  : bukrs;
   status     : abap.char(1);
   vendor     : Lifnrs
   plant      : werks_d;
   _itms      : composition [1..*] of ZI_PO_ITS;
}
@EndUserText.label: 'PO Items'
define custom entity ZI_PO_ITS
// with parameters parameter_name : parameter_type
{
   key po num    : ebeln;
   key po_item   : ebelp;
   item_text     : abap.char (40);
   material      : abap.char(40);
   plant         : werks_d;
   stor_loc      : Lgort_d;
   @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'uom
   qty           : abap.quan(13,3);
   uom           : abap.unit (3);
   @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'price_unit'
   product_price : abap.curr(10,2);
   price_unit    : abap.cuky;
   _HD           : association to parent ZI_PO_HEAD
                   on $projection.po_num = _hd.po_num;
}

Creating the Query Implementation Class

The runtime of a CDS custom entity must be implemented manually.

It requires an ABAP class that implements the select method of the interface IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER to handle query requests by the client.

This class is referenced by the custom entity and is dedicated to implementing the OData client data requests.

CLASS zcl_po_hd DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

PUBLIC SECTION.
 
  INTERFACES if rap_query_provider. 
PROTECTED SECTION.
PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_po_hd IMPLEMENTATION.

METHOD if_rap_query_provider~select.
ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Adding Query Implementation Class to the CDS Custom Entities- Header and Items

@EndUserText.label: 'PO Header' 
@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_PO_HD'
define root custom entity ZI_PO_HEAD
//with parameters parameter_name: parameter_type
{
   key po_num : ebeln;
   doc_cat    : bstyp;
   type       : abap.char(2);
   org        : ekorg;
   comp_code  : bukrs;
   status     : abap.char(1);
   vendor     : Lifnrs
   plant      : werks_d;
   _itms      : composition [1..*] of ZI_PO_ITS;
}
@EndUserText.label: 'PO Items'
@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_PO_HD
define custom entity ZI_PO_ITS
// with parameters parameter_name : parameter_type
{
   key po num    : ebeln;
   key po_item   : ebelp;
   item_text     : abap.char (40);
   material      : abap.char(40);
   plant         : werks_d;
   stor_loc      : Lgort_d;
   @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'uom
   qty           : abap.quan(13,3);
   uom           : abap.unit (3);
   @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'price_unit'
   product_price : abap.curr(10,2);
   price_unit    : abap.cuky;
   _HD           : association to parent ZI_PO_HEAD
                   on $projection.po_num = _hd.po_num;
}

Implementation

CLASS zcl_po_hd DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

PUBLIC SECTION.
 
  INTERFACES if rap_query_provider. 
PROTECTED SECTION.
PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_po_hd IMPLEMENTATION.

METHOD if_rap_query_provider~select.
 
 DATA: it_hd TYPE TABLE OF zi_po_head, 
       it_itms TYPE TABLE OF zi_po_its.

 CASE io_request-get_entity_id( ).

  WHEN 'ZI_PO_HEAD'
     
     DATA(lv_hd_fs) = io_request-get_filter( )->get_as_sql_string( ). 
     DATA(lo_paging) = io_request->get_paging( ).
     DATA(lv_skip) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_offset( ).
     DATA(lv_top) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_page_size( ).
     If lv_top < 0.
         lv_top = 1.
     ENDIF
      
     DATA(it_sort) = io_request->get_sort_elements( ).
     READ TABLE it_sort INTO DATA(ls_sort) INDEX 1.
     IF ls_sort-descending = abap_true.
        DATA(lv_order) = |'{ ls_sort-element_name } DESCENDING'|.
     ELSE.
         lv_order = |'{ ls_sort-element_name } ASCENDING'|.
     ENDIF.
     
     IF lv_order IS INITIAL.
      lv_order = 'po_num'.
     ENDIF.

     SELECT * FROM ztb_po_header
              WHERE (lv_hd_fs)
              ORDER BY (lv_order)
              INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE _hd.
     
      io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( it_hd ) ).
      
      IF lv_hd_fs IS INITIAL.
       IF lv_skip EQ 0.
        DELETE it_hd FROM lv_top + 1.
       ELSE.
        DELETE it_hd FROM lv_top + lv_skip + 1 TO lines( it_hd ).
        DELETE it_hd FROM 1 TO lv_skip.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
      
      io_response->set_data( it_hd ).

     
   WHEN 'ZI_PO_ITS'
     
      DATA(lv_item_fs) = io_request-get_filter( )->get_as_sql_string( ). 
      DATA(lv_skips) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_offset( ).
      DATA(lv_tops) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_page_size( ).
      If lv_top < 0.
         lv_top = 1.
      ENDIF
      

     SELECT * FROM ztb_po_item
              WHERE (lv_item_fs)
              ORDER BY po_num
              INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE _itms.
     
      io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( it_itms ) ).
      
      io_response->set_data( it_itms ).

   ENDCASE.


ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

References

  • Custom Entities | SAP Help Portal

  • CDS DDL - DEFINE CUSTOM ENTITY, ABAP Class | ABAP Keyword Documentation
  • Using a CDS Custom Entity for Data Modeling | SAP Help Portal

Conclusion

A CDS custom entity is used to write the custom logic with an ABAP class, and the data retrieval logic is  executed on the application server .

Service definition and Binding can be created for a custom entity with a Binding type Web API and UI.

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Integration with Qualtrics - Part 2: Automating the New Hire Survey
entry.content:
In this blog, we'll configure an event-driven integration that automatically triggers a New Hire Survey when a new employee joins the organization. No manual participant uploads, Just a seamless, automated experience.

What You'll Learn

  • How to set up an event-based trigger using the Intelligent Services Center (ISC)
  • How to configure the Manage Qualtrics Integrations screen for email surveys
  • How the integration connects to the Lifecycle Project in Qualtrics
  • Why the Global Directory from Part 1 is essential for this to work

Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure the following are in place:

 

#PrerequisiteDetails
1Part 1 CompletedEmployee directory sync is active and employees are flowing into the Qualtrics EX Global Directory
2Qualtrics Lifecycle ProjectThe Qualtrics consultant has created the New Hire Onboarding Survey as a Lifecycle Project and shared the Project ID
3Register Qualtrics in SuccessFactorsQualtrics authentication must be enabled in the Integration Service Registration Center. This is a one-time setup that allows SuccessFactors to communicate with Qualtrics.
4OAuth ConfigurationQualtrics API credentials (OAuth client) are configured in SuccessFactors
5Admin PermissionsAccess to Intelligent Services Center, Manage Qualtrics Integrations, and Integration Center in SuccessFactors

Architecture Overview

The flow works like this:

  1. A new hire is created in Employee Central
  2. The Intelligent Services Center/ISC detects the new hire event and waits 7 days after the start date
  3. After 7 days, the Manage Qualtrics Integrations email survey integration fires
  4. The integration sends the employee identifier to Qualtrics via API
  5. Qualtrics looks up the employee in the Global Directory (synced in Part 1)
  6. If found, the employee is added as a participant to the Lifecycle Project
  7. The survey invitation is sent automatically.

Step-by-Step Configuration

Step 1: Enable Qualtrics Authentication in Integration Service Registration Center

This is a one-time setup that allows SuccessFactors to communicate with the Qualtrics platform. If this has already been done for your instance, you can skip this step.

Navigation: Admin Center → Integration Service Registration Center

Step 2: Verify the Lifecycle Project in Qualtrics

Before configuring anything in SuccessFactors, confirm with your Qualtrics consultant that:

  • The Lifecycle Project (New Hire Onboarding Survey) has been created
  • The Project ID has been shared with you (e.g., TS_XXXXXXXXXX)
  • The survey is designed, tested, and ready to receive participants
  • The invitation email template and reminder settings are configured within the project

Note: The survey design, email templates, reminder schedule, and dashboard are all configured by the Qualtrics consultant within the Qualtrics platform. This blog focuses on the SAP SuccessFactors side of the integration.

Step 3: Create a Business Rule and Configure the Intelligent Services Center (ISC)

The Intelligent Services Center is where you define what event should trigger the integration and when it should fire. But first, you need to create a business rule that captures the new hire event.

Step 3a: Create the Business Rule

Before configuring the ISC event, you need to create a business rule that identifies when a new hire event occurs in Employee Central.

Navigation: Admin Center → Configure Business Rules

  1. Create a new business rule
  2. Define the rule to capture the hire event from Employee Central
  3. This rule acts as the filter - it tells the ISC exactly which employee events should trigger the survey integration

SAP Help Reference: For detailed steps on creating the business rule for email surveys, refer to the official SAP documentation: Creating Business Rule for Email Surveys

Tip: The business rule defines the "what" (which events to capture), while the ISC event subscription defines the "when" (timing of the trigger). Both work together to ensure the right employees receive the survey at the right time.

Step 3b: Configure the ISC Event Subscription

Once the business rule is created, configure the event subscription in the Intelligent Services Center.

Navigation: Admin Center → Intelligent Services Center

  1. Create a new event subscription
  2. Configure the following:

Setting Value

CategoryEmployee Central: Employee Recruitment
SourceIntegration Center
Timing7 Days After Effective Date
Business Rule(The business rule created in Step 3a)

       3. Save the event rule

 

What this does: The ISC monitors Employee Central for new hire events. When a new hire record is created and matches the business rule, the ISC doesn't fire immediately - it waits 7 days after the employee's effective start date before triggering the integration. This delay is configurable based on your organization's needs.

Step 4: Configure the Email Survey in Manage Qualtrics Integrations

This is where you create and configure the email survey integration that links the ISC event to the Qualtrics Lifecycle Project.

Navigation: Admin Center → Manage Qualtrics Integrations → Email Surveys

Click + Create New Integration and fill in the following:

#FieldValue
1Integration NameSF_NewHire
2Product AreaEmployee Central
3Event NameEmployee Recruitment
4Survey RecipientEmployee
5Business Scenario Name[Employee Central] Qualtrics: New Hire
6OAuth Configuration(Configured in Step 4b)
7Rule Configuration(Business rule from Step 3a)
8Survey Info - Project ID(Lifecycle Project ID from Qualtrics)

Fields 1–4 are self-explanatory. Let's look at the ones that need more detail:

Step 4a: Business Scenario Name

FieldValue
Business Scenario Name[Employee Central] Qualtrics: New Hire

This links the integration to the pre-delivered Integration Center business scenario that handles the data transfer to Qualtrics.

This is a standard pre-delivered business scenario. You don't need to create it - it becomes available automatically when the Qualtrics integration is enabled in your SuccessFactors instance.

Step 4b: OAuth Configuration

FieldValue
OAuth Configuration(Select your Qualtrics OAuth client)

The OAuth configuration provides the secure connection between SuccessFactors and your Qualtrics instance. This must be set up before you can activate the integration.

If the OAuth configuration has not been created yet:

SAP Help Reference: Follow the official SAP documentation for step-by-step instructions: Creating Outbound OAuth Configurations for Qualtrics APIs for Email Survey Integrations

Once created, return to the Edit Integration screen and select the OAuth configuration from the dropdown.

Step 4c: Rule Configuration

FieldValue
Rule Configuration(Select the business rule created in Step 3a)

This links the business rule you created in Step 2a. The rule acts as a filter - only events that match the rule (i.e., new hire events) will trigger the survey.

Step 4d: Survey Info - Project ID

FieldValue
Survey Info - Project IDTS_XXXXXXXXXX

This tells the integration which Qualtrics survey project should receive the participant.

Where to find the Project ID:

MethodHow
URL BarOpen the Lifecycle Project in Qualtrics. The Project ID is in the URL: https://yourorg.qualtrics.com/xxxx/ProjectID=TS_XXXXXXXXXX

Important: Use the Lifecycle Project ID, not a regular survey ID. The integration is designed to work with Qualtrics Lifecycle or Employee Engagement projects.

Step 4e: Review and Activate

Step 5: Activate the business scenario

This links the integration to the pre-delivered business scenario.

FieldValue
Business Scenario Name[Employee Central] Qualtrics: New Hire

Note: This is a standard pre-delivered business scenarios. You don't need to create it - it's automatically available when Qualtrics integration is enabled.

Step 6: Understand What Happens Behind the Scenes

Once the integration is active, here's what happens every time a new hire is created:

Day 0

  • New hire record created in Employee Central

Day 0–1

  • Part 1 daily sync adds the employee to the Qualtrics EX Global Directory

Day 7

  • ISC event fires based on the business rule
  • Integration sends the employee identifier to Qualtrics
  • Qualtrics looks up the employee in the Global Directory
  • Employee is added as a participant to the Lifecycle Project
  • Survey invitation email is sent to the new hire

Key Dependency: The employee must exist in the Qualtrics Global Directory before the Day 7 trigger fires. This is why Part 1 (Employee Directory Sync) is a critical prerequisite. The 7-day delay provides a natural buffer - the daily sync ensures the employee is in the directory well before Day 7.

Step 7: Verify the Integration

After activation, test the integration with a test employee:

  • Create a test new hire in Employee Central (or use an existing test employee in a sandbox)

             Employee was hired on 2nd March.

  • Wait for Part 1 sync to confirm the employee appears in the Qualtrics Global Directory
  • Trigger the ISC event (in a test environment, you may be able to trigger it manually without waiting 7 days)

  • Check the Lifecycle Project in Qualtrics to confirm the employee appears as a participant

  • Check the email to confirm the survey invitation was received
CheckpointWhat to verify
Global DirectoryEmployee appears with correct attributes
Lifecycle ProjectEmployee listed as a participant
Email InboxSurvey invitation received
Survey LinkSurvey opens correctly with personalized content
  • If the employee doesn't exist in the global directory, the sync will fail with below error.

What Does the Qualtrics Consultant Configure?

While this blog covers the SAP SuccessFactors side, it's helpful to understand what happens on the Qualtrics side. The Qualtrics consultant is responsible for:

Survey DesignQuestions, logic, branding
Invitation EmailEmail template with personalized fields (name, department, etc.) pulled from the Global Directory
RemindersAutomatic follow-up emails if the employee hasn't completed the survey (e.g., after 3 days, 7 days)
Survey ExpirationHow long the survey stays open
DashboardReporting and analytics for HR leaders
AnonymityConfidentiality and access control settings

Reminders are configured entirely within the Qualtrics Lifecycle Project. Once a participant is added by our integration, Qualtrics manages the full survey lifecycle - invitation, reminders, expiration - automatically.

Conclusion

This integration between SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central and Qualtrics automates the onboarding survey process — ensuring every new hire receives timely feedback requests without manual intervention.

Key Takeaways

  • Automation - Eliminates manual survey distribution using ISC rules and Integration Center
  • Timeliness - Captures new hire sentiment at the right moment
  • Accuracy - Employee data flows directly from EC to Qualtrics with no manual entry
  • Scalability - Works seamlessly irrespective of hire counts
  • Error Handling - Always account for edge cases like missing participants in Qualtrics

By combining the power of SAP SuccessFactors Integration Center, Intelligent Services, and Qualtrics, organizations can build a seamless, data-driven onboarding experience - capturing valuable employee feedback from Day 1.

Thanks for taking time to read..!

Coming Up Next

Blog 3: SAP SuccessFactors Integration with Qualtrics - Part 3: LMS Learning/Training Feedback

We'll explore how to trigger training feedback surveys automatically when an employee completes a learning course in SuccessFactors Learning - capturing real-time insights to continuously improve your L&D programs.

Stay tuned!

entry.title: Re: Week 2 : Use Joule Chat to create ABAP RAP Application and explore the Explain feature
Re: Cross Deployment Unit Issue – Cross-DU Issue: Auto Cr...
entry.content:
Hello Pr789,

I noticed that from the Customer.xbo - BeforeSave event you attempt to create an instance on the custom BO Account_to_Ticket_IC.
You also have created an internal communication (.pid) related to this custom BO.

Now,  I don't know which BOs are involved in the .pid, but my assumption would be that the Sending BO is Customer and the receiving BO is Account_to_Ticket_IC. If that's the case, you don't need the logic on Customer.xbo - BeforeSave, since any time a Prospect is created, a new record will be created in the custom BO Account_to_Ticket_IC due to the internal communication.

You can then move the logic from Customer.xbo - BeforeSave to the Account_to_Ticket_IC BO, in order to create a new standard ticket from the Custom BO.

In Summary:
1. When a prospect is created, it should be replicated to Account_to_Ticket_IC BO via the .pid
2. A new ticket gets created for the customer due to the logic written in Account_to_Ticket_IC BO 
3. You might need to have some logic to not create more than one ticket for the same prospect if further changes are performed on prospect instance.

Another suggestion which works really well, is to follow the approach from below blog post:
https://blog.acorel.nl/2018/05/cross-deployment-unit-object-creation-businesspartner-lead/

It will require the creation of two custom BOs which will act as Sender and Receiver, and an internal communication (.pid) between the two. If your PDI solution was created in the Deployment Unit CRM (you can check this by right clicking the solution > solution properties), then the Sender BO should have the annotation [DeploymentUnit (Foundation)]. But if the PDI solution Deployment Unit chosen was Foundation, then the Receiver BO should have the annotation [DeploymentUnit (Customer Relationship Management)]

For your case, when a prospect is created, the logic from Customer.xbo Before Save will create a record in the Sender BO, of which will replicate to the Receiver BO via the .pid. In the Receiver BO, you can place the logic to finally create the actual ticket!

It might be interesting to add a validation in the Customer.xbo BeforeSave script to only create the ticket once and for that you can add an indicator field like "element Indicator : Indicator = false;" on Customer.xbo and in the BeforeSave script check if "!this.Indicator". If false, then you can proceed with the Sender BO instance creation and update the Indicator = true at the end, to avoid new tickets from being created again for same customer.

Please let me know if this works well for you.

Best regards,
Thiago

entry.title: Re: allowing negative stock in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hello Houssam,

  1. Is it possible to allow negative stock in EWM ?  If yes, is it possible for a selected storage type to allow negative quantities? - Yes, not at Warehouse level and only at storage type level.
  2. what are the impacts ? - Although SAP EWM technically allows negative stock, it is not recommended. If negative stock is enabled, you need to understand that it has downstream impacts across multiple areas, including delivery processing, production execution, and annual or cycle physical inventory. Also, in landscapes with MES/MFS integration, negative stock can also impact synchronization, deadlocks and production consumption processes. 

Thank you,

Yogesh

entry.title: 318864/2026 の続き
entry.content:
DIGITS/DIGITS でのご回答ありがとうございます。

誤ってケースを終了してしまったので、こちらで改めて.msgファイルをお送りします。

ご確認お願いします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 318864/2026 の続き
entry.content:
添付されたメールを確認いたしました。このメールはケースの更新通知に関するものです。 したがって、私の解決策は、以前のケース担当者の解決策と一致しています。

*が今後ケース関連の通知を受け取りたい場合は、以下の2つのシナリオがあります：

新規作成されたケースの場合：

今後担当者*が新しいケースを作成し、ケースのreporterとなることで、*はその後ケースの通知を受け取ることができます。

既存ケースの場合：

既存ケースに*をKBA 2213199 - How to add/remove additional contacts in an existing case - SAP for Meによってadditional contactとして追加すれば、現在オープン状態にあるケースが更新された際に、*もメール通知を受け取ることができます。

ただし、KBAの関連内容によると「Additional contacts must be added to each case separately (e.g. there is not a way to add a default contact for all cases).」によりますと、各ケースの中で個別にadditional contactを追加する必要があります。

もし、私の理解が正確でない、またはご質問やご不明な点がございましたら、いつでもお気軽にお問い合わせください。

entry.title: Re: Company Code–Wise Number Range Configuration for PO
entry.content:
Purchase order number ranges are defined at the document type level, not at the company code level. This means you can configure different number ranges for different PO document types. However, the system does not support company code–specific number ranges in standard configuration. The format you requested (e.g., IN08000000000000 for company code IN08) cannot be achieved directly through standard number range settings.

You can explore the below possible alternate approaches:

1) Separate PO Document Types per Company Code
Create distinct PO document types for each company code. Assign unique number ranges to those document types. This way, each company code indirectly gets its own number range.

2) Custom Logic (BAdI)
If strict company code–wise numbering is mandatory, you can explore extensibility options (Custom Logic BAdI for number range determination). This enhancement can dynamically select the number range based on the company code.

3) Prefix in PO Output
Another option is to keep the standard number range but add the company code prefix (e.g., IN08) in the PO output form or communication layout, so users see the company code reflected in the PO number externally.

I started already implementing the "Flights" example from this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/create-graphs-on-sap-hana-cloud/ba-p/13456122 ... and it really worked 😊

If I take this Fligths graph:

create or replace graph workspace "Demo_Flights"

source column "AIRPORTCODEORIGIN"
target column "AIRPORTCODEDESTINATION"
key column "ROUTEID"

What is another difference, to regular reporting tables ... obviously the uses columns in the Graph must all have the setting "not null". entry.title: Re: Using local (Belgian) G/L account numbers vs. YCOA: w...
entry.content:
Good day @Wayne10 

Thank you for your questions.

The operating chart of accounts remains the basis for technical posting in Public Cloud. So, in your setup, YCOA stays the operational account structure. The alternative or local chart of accounts can be used in selected manual journal entry scenarios such as Post General Journal Entries, Manage Journal Entries, and Upload General Journal Entries, where SAP provides the relevant alternative account functionality.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/5a06325798d8682be10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/32ed3c6435ea46afb4dbab6839fabe8e.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/583f130d246a4c61ab954580fe21787a.html?locale=en-US

When you look at integrated and system determined processes, such as supplier invoices, MM driven postings (GR or IR, valuation driven postings, etc.), reclassification jobs, integrated asset postings, and automated accrual postings, the system continues to post against YCOA. In these cases, local account numbers may appear in value help or display but they are not the actual operational posting account.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb9f1469daf04bd894ab2167f8132a1a/46475e69bf954f168ea95a064fba0eb8.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/72256b54f94c8f4ce10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/f990dea8cd044f5fb9f2b6fcade0f805.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/c96421d8497b4df782ee75b7ee81fef4.html?locale=en-US

So as a rule of thumb for a project in Public Cloud, I suggest you use local Belgian account numbers as an aid for manual entry and display but treat YCOA as the effective posting chart for operational transaction.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Service - Warranty Process - 6 months fre...
entry.content: Create a warranty service contract with 6-month validity and item category as Not Relevant for Billing. This ensures any repair service order linked to the contract is automatically billed at zero during the warranty period, without needing custom enhancements.

entry.title: Re: TDS amount not shown in supplier line item report
entry.content:
Why TDS shows in journal entry & TDS report but not in Manage Supplier line items is because, Supplier Line Item App (F0997A) is designed to show vendor open items and clearings based on supplier account postings. TDS postings are often made to tax accounts (with vendor line item cleared or adjusted separately). Depending on how the posting keys and account determination are set, the TDS component may not appear as a separate vendor line item. In contrast, the TDS report reads directly from withholding tax tables (e.g., BSET, BSEG), so it correctly shows the deduction. If the vendor line item is net of TDS, the gross vs. tax split may not be visible in the supplier line item report.

Having said that the system is working as designed and the TDS is correctly deducted and reflected in journal entries and tax reports. The “Manage Supplier Line Items” app does not always display TDS separately because those postings are made to tax accounts, not vendor accounts. If you need vendor-level visibility of TDS in the supplier line item report, you would need either:

  • A custom analytical report combining vendor line items with withholding tax tables, or
  • Use the Withholding Tax Items – India app for tax-specific reconciliation.

entry.title: Restriction on GL Account and Cost Center in Manage Service Entry Sheet App
entry.content:
Restriction on GL Account and Cost Center in Manage Service Entry Sheet App

Hello Experts,

We are trying to enforce restrictions on GL Account and Cost Center combinations in the Fiori Manage Service Entry Sheet (SES) app so that invalid combinations are blocked before the SES can be saved.

However, we observed that in the current SES processing flow, validation logic is not always executed before the data is persisted. In some cases, the validation error is raised after the data has already been committed, which allows restricted GL or Cost Center values to be saved.

We explored several options but faced limitations:

  • BAdI MM_PUR_S4_SES_CHECK_ITEM
    This BAdI provides header and item data but does not expose account assignment data (SESACCOUNTASSIGNMENT) where the GL and Cost Center information is stored.

  • BAdI FAGL_SEGMENT_BS_ACC
    This is triggered during FI posting, but not all GL accounts trigger it, making it unreliable for SES validation.

We also attempted the following:

  • Validation during SES creation, copy, and change scenarios

  • Reading CDS views

  • Calling FI_CHECK_ACCOUNT_ASSIGNMENT for GL + Cost Center validation

  • Extending account assignment data via the Custom Logic app

However, the available enhancement points in the SES flow do not consistently execute before the save, making it difficult to reliably block invalid combinations. Additionally, some relevant BAdIs appear to be SAP-internal and not open for customer implementation.

Our current framework version also does not expose the GL or Cost Center parameters that seem to exist in newer SES versions.

Has anyone successfully implemented a reliable validation or restriction for GL Account and Cost Center combinations in the Fiori SES app before saving?

Any guidance or recommended enhancement point would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Tax rate not calculating for intergration with vertex...
entry.content:
Good day @Thiru2203 

Thank you for your questions.

Question: What could cause a migrated material to be treated as non-taxable by Vertex even though it appears to have similar attributes?

Answer: Based on your description, the issue you are facing is probably not the scheduling agreement itself, but a product master data difference between the material you created and the one you migrated.

The Product master migration object in Public Cloud includes a dedicated Tax Classifications section and for Tax Data (Sales) you must provide country, Tax category and Tax classification. Note that in Manage Product Master Data for a product master record, you should specify the Tax Classification for Sales Tax.

So, if you migrated Material B without the same tax classification values that exist on Material A, Vertex can receive the item with a different taxability interpretation and return it as non taxable.

Question: Which material fields are required for tax determination when using the Vertex tax engine?

Answer: For your scenario, the most relevant material fields you should check first to compare are the product’s sales tax classification fields in the sales area. So, you should ensure in your product master that:

  1. Sales area extension exists for the same sales org and distribution channel.
  2. You maintained Tax Classification for Sales Tax.
  3. In your migration terms, the product has the correct Country, Tax Category and Tax Classification in the Tax Classification section.

Consult these references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/289644d401a844878ce84670517dfa98.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/0483875792f25c08e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

Keep in mind that you can only provide tax classification data for a sales organisation that you newly create. It is not supported to update existing sales organisations in the migration object. This is important because a common root cause is:

  • The product was migrated,
  • The sales org extension exists, but the relevant tax classification data was not loaded correctly or was not loaded for the required sales org setup.

So, if you created Material B through migration and later extended differently, then you should check this restriction carefully.

Your duplicate text is very useful. Since your manually created product works, and a copy of that manual product also works, while the migrated product does not, that strongly points to a difference in master data difference on the product’s sales tax relevant data, not a general problem with Vertex connectivity, tax category O1, the scheduling agreement process itself or the overall tax procedure.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/0483875792f25c08e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

Please use Manage Product Master Data to compare the two products side by side and check at least in the:

  • Sales area (same sales organisation, same distribution channel and same Tax Classification for Sales Tax)
  • In your migration content (same country, same tax category and same tax classification in the Tax Classifications sheet or structure).
  • In your organisational setup, confirm the migrated product is actually extended to the same sales area in which you are creating the scheduling agreement.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/9c62e3f7092744d49021051301263e85.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Refill Plan in S/4HANA Cross-Catalog Mapping
entry.content: In S 4HANA the Refill Plan is usually not assigned through Cross-Catalog Mapping like it was in CRM. The refill or recurring logic is typically handled through SAP Convergent Charging. You need to configure the refill behavior in Convergent Charging and link it to the subscription products charge or rate plan. The subscription product then uses that charging configuration to manage the refill cycle.

entry.title: Hello SAP community! My name is Shlok Shigli and I'm excited to start my journey
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community Post – Shlok Shigli

Introduction

Hello SAP Community! 👋

My name is Shlok Shigli, and I am currently pursuing Mechanical Engineering at REVA University in Bangalore, India.

I’m excited to be part of the Hello SAP Community initiative. This opportunity allows beginners like me to explore the SAP ecosystem, learn from experienced professionals, and connect with people across the global SAP network.

As a student, I am passionate about learning new technologies and understanding how SAP solutions help businesses manage operations more efficiently. Through the SAP Community, I look forward to gaining insights from experts, discovering new ideas, and building meaningful professional connections.

I’m excited to share my learning journey and contribute to the SAP Community as I continue to grow.

Thank you, SAP Community, for this wonderful opportunity!

1. What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to build a successful career at the intersection of technology and engineering. I want to continuously learn new skills and work on innovative solutions that help organizations improve efficiency and make better decisions. I also hope to contribute to the tech community and grow as a professional in the global technology ecosystem.

2. Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In the next five years, I see myself as a skilled technology professional with strong technical knowledge and hands-on experience. I hope to work on real-world projects, continuously improve my expertise, and contribute positively to my organization and the wider technology community.

3. What is your hobby?

One of my hobbies is exploring new technologies and learning about innovations in engineering and business systems. I enjoy reading, learning new skills online, and continuously expanding my knowledge through different learning platforms.

4. Which 5 things do you absolutely want to achieve in life?

  • Build a strong career in the technology and engineering field
  • Continuously improve myself by learning new technologies and skills
  • Work on projects that create real-world impact
  • Build meaningful connections and collaborate with talented people
  • Maintain a balanced life while pursuing personal and professional growth

5. What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

One of the most important lessons my parents taught me is to stay disciplined and never stop learning. They always reminded me that progress takes time and consistency. Their guidance has helped me stay focused on my goals and approach challenges with patience and determination.

My Experience at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru

SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2026 – Excited to be part of this amazing SAP Community eventSAP campus in Bengaluru

Recently, I had the opportunity to attend SAP Inside Track Bengaluru, and it was an incredibly valuable learning experience for me as a student exploring the SAP ecosystem.

Being part of an event where SAP professionals and community members shared their knowledge was very inspiring. It helped me better understand how SAP technologies are used to solve real-world business problems and improve organizational efficiency.

One of the most interesting parts of the event was interacting with SAP experts and fellow learners. Listening to their experiences and insights gave me a broader perspective on the different opportunities within the SAP ecosystem.

An insightful session on Generative AI and AI Agents,

Key Takeaways from the Event

  • The growing importance of AI and intelligent technologies in enterprise solutions
  • How SAP platforms help organizations improve efficiency and business processes
  • The strong role of the SAP Community in sharing knowledge and supporting learners.

I’m excited to continue learning and sharing my journey with the SAP Community as I explore more about SAP technologies

entry.title: Re: Issues Related to Down Payment in Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hello @chhatrapal_mahajanacc82e,

Please see below some answers to your questions:

1) When I add a down payment at the header level in the Purchase Order, the amount is not reflecting while creating the Down Payment Request.

By “the amount is not reflecting”, do you mean that the amount is not proposed while you are creating the Down Payment Request? If yes, this is the expected system behavior, please see here: 2894701 - Down payment amount in PO header not proposed when creating Down Payment Request - SAP for Me. If you want to have a proposed value in "Create Down Payment Request" you have to define the down payment on item level in the PO.

2) If a down payment of ₹10 is maintained in the PO, the system is still allowing the creation of a Down Payment Request for more than ₹10.

Because of the behaviour described under 1 the down payment value maintained at the PO header does not control, limit, or validate the down payment request amount. Therefore, the system also allows the Down Payment Request to exceed the amount maintained at header level.

3) When I use the “Monitor Purchase Order Down Payment Requests” tile, the workflow is not getting triggered/working.

When you use Monitor Purchase Order Down Payments you can use the button “Create Down Payment Request”, which should navigate you to the Manage Supplier Down Payment Requests app. If this navigation is not working, it could be due to some missing authorization or due to a system error. In this case, opening a case in https://me.sap.com/home might be helpful for you.

Best regards,

Kerisha
